checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains text that matches the source document. The section titles 'AGENDA' and 'Agenda Page 2' are appropriate structural additions for document navigation and accessibility. All content elements in the output have a clear basis in the original PDF text.
I verified the phone number '984-204-1487' and the Conference ID '766 174 851#' against the source document. Both are present and identical to the original meeting information provided in the header.

All agenda items from page 1 (items 1-6 and pre-numbered items) and page 2 (items 7-13, Adjourn, and closing note) are present in the output JSON. The page break is correctly handled by creating a new section for the second page's content.

The source document contains one primary table at the top of page 1. The output JSON represents this table with 8 rows (1 header row and 7 data rows), which matches the visual structure of the original PDF exactly.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A thorough comparison of the detailed financial items under 'Consent Agenda' (item 5) confirms that all dollar amounts and fund names were captured correctly. For example, PSAP Fund/Account balances and disbursements match perfectly. No substantial omissions were found in any section.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output JSON accurately reflects the source document. Minor formatting choices, such as including leader dots in the text or normalizing the list structure, do not alter the meaning of the content. OCR accuracy is extremely high for names and numbers.

checks.a_analysis: The content from both pages of the source PDF appears only once in the JSON output. Header/footer information is either correctly placed in metadata or omitted, preventing duplication.

The JSON uses a level 2 heading for the main meeting title. While there is no H1 tag (the document title is in metadata), starting at H2 is an acceptable normalization for screen reader navigation within sections. No levels are skipped (e.g., H2 to H4).

The source document presents the entire agenda (from 'Call to Order' through item 13) in a three-column tabular format (Tab, Topic, Presenter). The JSON output correctly identifies items 1-4 as a table but truncates the table structure thereafter. Items 5 through 13 are converted into standard paragraphs and lists, merging the 'Topic' and 'Presenter' column data into single text strings. This destroys the structural relationship between agenda items and their assigned presenters.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 and 6 on Page 1, and Items 7-13 on Page 2, align with the 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' column headers.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the original agenda document across both pages.
Nested lists (specifically 5a-5f and 6a-6g) are correctly identified and nested in the JSON, preserving their hierarchical relationship despite the table structure issue noted in S3.
No links were injected into the document. The phone number and conference ID are correctly represented as text strings.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF maintains a consistent, unified columnar layout for all agenda items (1-13), with distinct columns for 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'. The HTML output breaks this consistency by rendering items 1-4 in a structured table, but then switching to mixed list and paragraph formats for items 5-13. This fragmenting of the document's primary structure significantly changes its visual organization. Furthermore, in Item 5 (sections b-e), multiple lines of financial data from the source (Header, Balance, Disbursement) are collapsed into single continuous lines of text in the HTML, which severely degrades the readability and logical grouping of the figures.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5b in the source PDF lists 'NG 911 Fund/Account', 'April 2020 Month-End Balance', and 'April 2020 Disbursement' on separate lines with aligned dollar amounts. Items 7-13 are visually aligned with the 'Presenter' column on the right.
checks.b_evidence_output: Item 5b is rendered as a single line: '2. b) NG 911 Fund/Account April 2020 Month-End Balance $ 66,897,707 April 2020 Disbursement $ 65,036'. Items 7-13 lose their columnar presenter alignment and use plain text dot leaders instead.

checks.a_analysis: The agenda items in the source PDF are presented in a tabular format with defined headers ('Tab', 'Topic', 'Presenter'). While the HTML output correctly initiates a table for the first few items, it terminates the table prematurely after item 4. Items 5 through 13, which are part of the same tabular structure in the source, are rendered as plain text paragraphs and lists. Specifically, items 7-13 are rendered as plain text with manual dot leaders, failing the requirement to maintain tabular data structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: The entire agenda (items 1-13) is organized under the headers 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table only contains items up to '4. Ceremonial Swearing-In...'. Items 7-13 are rendered as: '7. Education Committee Report.....................................Chuck Greene/Angie Turbeville'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Items 5 through 13; second page of HTML rendering.

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful images from the source PDF, specifically the 'NC DIT 911 Board' logos appearing on both pages, are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output in their respective logical sections.

No body content, paragraphs, or tables are visually duplicated in the HTML output. The repetition of the document logo and the inclusion of a 'Agenda Page 2' header are appropriate for conveying the structure of the original two-page document and do not constitute content duplication.
